title: sambucus nigra subsp. cerulea
intro: Sambucus nigra subsp. cerulea, commonly known as blue elderberry, is a subspecies of the elder tree that is native to North America. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds that can help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms. The health benefits of sambucus nigra subsp. cerulea include its ability to lower fever, reduce congestion, and alleviate sore throats, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues. Sambucus nigra subsp. cerulea can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be purchased over-the-counter or made at home using the plant's berries and flowers.
